data.description:

  • Forward yield 1.68%
  • Payable Aug. 27; for shareholders of record Aug. 12; ex-div Aug. 12.
  • See DCI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Intel confirms plans to spin off its networking communications business
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) confirmed plans to spin off its networking and communications business as a standalone company. 

Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) has begun the process of identifying strategic investors for the business, a spokesman confirmed to CNBC on Friday.

"We plan to establish key elements of our Networking and Communications business as a stand-alone company and we have begun the process of identifying strategic investors," an Intel spokesperson told CNBC. "Like Altera, we will remain an anchor investor enabling us to benefit from future upside as we position the business for future growth.

News of the spinoff was earlier reported by CRN.

Intel on Thursday said it's planning to cut 15% of jobs and will end the year with a global headcount of ~75,000 as a part of sweeping restructuring under new CEO Lip-Bu Tan.

Shares of Intel (INTC) sunk 9% on Friday as the semiconductor firm continues to find its footing in foundry while losing market share to rivals such as AMD (AMD).

data.title: OceanFirst signals net interest income growth, targets $500M Premier Bank deposits by year-end while expanding commercial pipeline
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: OceanFirst Financial Corp. (OCFC) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Christopher D. Maher, Chairman and CEO, described the quarter as an investment period, highlighting the addition of C&I bankers, the launch of Premier Bank, and the opening of new offices in Melville, NY and Perth Amboy, NJ. Maher noted, "Revenue growth has been on a strongly positive track, and we expect that to continue, while absolute expenses remain flat with some potential to decrease over time. As a result, we view the quarter as a trough in EPS that we will build from this point as the organic growth momentum continues."
  • Maher reported net interest income growth for the third consecutive quarter, with a $1 million increase and a 1 basis point expansion in net interest margin. He emphasized, "The loan growth in the quarter came late in June. So the quarterly results don't fully reflect the earnings power of the balance sheet, which is better positioned for additional improvements to net interest income in the third quarter."
  • Total loans increased $60 million, with commercial and industrial loans rising 8%. Operating expenses were $71 million, aligning with expectations and previous guidance. Asset quality remained strong with special mention and substandard loans at $145 million, or 1.4% of total loans.
  • Maher mentioned a $17 million share repurchase (1 million shares at a weighted average cost of $17.17) and the redemption of $57 million in preferred stock. A new authorization for 3 million additional shares was approved on July 15.
  • The company’s commercial pipeline reached a record $791 million. Maher stated, "We expect an increase in net interest income in the third quarter and continued improvement to margins in the second half of the year."
  • Joseph J. Lebel, Senior EVP & COO, explained that loan originations totaled $716 million, including $426 million from the commercial bank and $232 million in C&I loans. Lebel said, "At this point, we've completed the majority of our commercial banking hires for the year with 13 C&I bankers and 36 premier bankers hired in 2025."
  • Lebel reported that the new premier banking teams brought in $115 million in deposits across more than 670 accounts, with a $500 million deposit target by year-end.
  • Patrick S. Barrett, CFO, stated, "As Chris noted, both net interest income and margin grew in the quarter with loan yields increasing 4 basis points and total deposit costs remaining flat."

Outlook

  • Management expects positive expansion in both net interest income and margin in the back half of the year, based on period-end balances and pipelines.
  • Maher indicated, "We expect this progress to continue while credit performance remains among the best in our peer group."
  • Lebel explained that premier banking teams are on pace to reach the $500 million deposit target by year-end. Barrett shared guidance that operating expenses should remain stable in the $71 million to $72 million per quarter range.

Financial Results

  • OceanFirst reported earnings per share of $0.28 on a fully diluted GAAP basis and $0.31 on a core basis for Q2 2025.
  • Net interest income grew by $1 million, and the net interest margin expanded by 1 basis point. Total loans increased by $60 million, with C&I loans up 8% for the quarter.
  • Noninterest income increased 5% to $11.8 million; after excluding noncore items, it was down 1% due to lower swap activity.
  • Operating expenses were $71.5 million, with compensation and professional fees rising due to new hires. Nonrecurring recruiting fees totaled $1.6 million.
  • The company repurchased 1 million shares and redeemed $57 million of preferred stock. Tangible book value per share was $19.34, and the common equity Tier 1 capital ratio was 11%.

Q&A

  • Daniel Tamayo, Raymond James, asked about deposit cost trends and margin trajectory. Maher responded, "There is certainly opportunity to mix shift, to reduce it a little bit. I think absent rate cut, I wouldn't see a lot of movement in the near term."
  • Tamayo inquired about margin expansion, to which Maher replied, "We are in slow and steady process, where it's going to just come up maybe a few basis points a quarter."
  • Timothy Jeffrey Switzer, KBW, asked about the base for noninterest income guidance. Barrett confirmed, "GAAP is the best base to use. They're almost the same at this point for this quarter."
  • Switzer inquired about expense impact from new hires; Maher stated, "The additional expenses in Q2 probably hit us about $0.06 in EPS."
  • David Jason Bishop, Hovde Group, questioned the potential for deposit cost reduction as premier accounts ramp. Maher noted, "Our expectation is that 30% or so will be noninterest-bearing. The rest is going to be some version of market...very efficient funding, but we don't expect it to be free funding."
  • Bishop also asked about C&I loan growth; Lebel said, "We're seeing it all over the footprint...we've seen good continued momentum in our Northern Virginia market and government contracting."
  • Manuel Antonio Navas, D.A. Davidson, asked about sustainability of loan growth. Lebel stated, "I think we feel pretty confident given the pipeline that we have...I think this is a sustainable growth rate."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was generally neutral to slightly positive, with questions focused on expense management, deposit funding, and margin expansion. Analysts challenged management on the timing and sustainability of growth initiatives.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks and Q&A. Maher and Barrett provided direct answers, expressing optimism about loan growth, deposit gathering, and margin improvement. Phrases such as "we expect" and "we are in slow and steady process" indicate measured confidence. In response to challenges, management used clarifying language but avoided defensiveness.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was similarly upbeat, but this call included more specifics on growth targets and run-rate stability, suggesting increased confidence.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from general optimism in Q1 to specific targets and run-rates in Q2, including the $500 million deposit goal for the Premier Bank and stable $71 million to $72 million quarterly expenses.
  • Strategic focus moved from early-stage hiring and deposit gathering to execution and maximizing productivity from new hires.
  • Analysts in Q2 probed more on expense sustainability and deposit cost trends, while Q1 focused on hiring plans and balance sheet remixing.
  • Key metrics such as net interest income and margin continued to improve, though EPS declined from $0.35 in Q1 to $0.28 (GAAP) in Q2, reflecting the investment phase.
  • Management’s confidence in deposit gathering and commercial loan pipeline increased, and analysts’ tone remained constructive but pressed for clarity on future expense containment and funding costs.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the risk of higher expenses due to new hires, but expects stabilization. Barrett noted, "We actually do think there are opportunities for us in absolute terms to gain some additional expense efficiencies."
  • Seasonality in deposits remains a challenge, with Q2 typically the low point.
  • The potential impact of interest rate cuts on margin was discussed, but management considers the exposure manageable. Barrett stated, "We're not wildly exposed to much volatility with or without Fed rate cuts."
  • Credit quality risks remain low, but management monitors the mix shift toward C&I loans for potential reserve changes.

Final Takeaway

OceanFirst Financial Corp. highlighted Q2 2025 as an investment period focused on expanding its commercial banking footprint and launching new initiatives like the Premier Bank, which is on track for $500 million in deposits by year-end. Management emphasized continued growth in net interest income and margin, stable expenses, and strong asset quality as key drivers for the back half of the year, positioning the company for improved earnings momentum as recent hires and business investments mature.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: First Hawaiian signals low single-digit loan growth outlook amid stabilized floor plan balances
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: First Hawaiian, Inc. (FHB) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robert Scott Harrison highlighted strong second quarter performance, citing, "Our net income increased over 23% compared to the prior quarter. The improvements in our results compared to the last quarter were broad-based driven by higher net interest and noninterest income, good expense control and lower provision expense." Harrison also noted, "We repurchased about 1 million shares at a total cost of $25 million. We had $50 million of remaining authorization under the approved 2025 stock repurchase plan."
  • Harrison outlined the economic backdrop, mentioning, "Statewide seasonally adjusted unemployment rate continued to drift lower and was 2.8% in June, compared to the national unemployment rate of 4.1%. Through May, total visitor arrivals were up 2.8% compared to last year as the strength in U.S. Mainland arrivals more than offset weakness in the Japanese and Canadian markets."
  • CFO James M. Moses stated, "Total deposits increased slightly in the second quarter as growth in public deposits more than offset the decline in commercial and retail deposits. Total deposit costs fell by 4 basis points in the quarter and our noninterest-bearing deposit ratio remained at 34%." Moses also noted, "Net interest income was $163.6 million, $3.1 million higher than the prior quarter and the NIM was 3.11% million up 3 basis points compared to the prior quarter."
  • Executive VP & Chief Risk Officer Lea M. Nakamura said, "The bank continues to maintain its strong credit performance and healthy credit metrics. Credit risk remains low, stable and well within our expectations."

Outlook

  • Harrison indicated a change in guidance: "Looking forward, we expect full year loan growth will be in the low single digits." This is a shift from previous expectations of low to mid-single digits.
  • Moses provided margin insights: "We anticipate that the NIM in the third quarter will increase a couple of basis points to 3.13%."
  • On expenses, Moses stated, "We think expenses in the third quarter will be up around 2% on a linked quarter basis and that full year expenses will be better than originally expected at around $506 million."

Financial Results

  • Net interest income was $163.6 million, an increase of $3.1 million from the prior quarter.
  • Noninterest income came in at $54 million, with Moses clarifying, "We continue to expect that recurring piece of noninterest income will be about $51 million per quarter."
  • Total loans increased by $59 million or 0.4% from the prior quarter, with the C&I portfolio seeing a $125 million increase in dealer floor plan balances.
  • Classified assets increased by $31.6 million, net charge-offs were $3.3 million for the quarter, and the allowance for credit losses rose by $1.2 million to $167.8 million.

Q&A

  • Liam Joseph Coohill, Raymond James: Asked about C&I pipeline and CRE demand. Harrison replied, "Most of the C&I growth came in our dealer floor plan...car sales have slowed a little bit, but still, there's uncertainty out there with respect to tariffs."
  • Coohill also inquired about capital priorities. Moses responded, "We'd love to deploy that in organic growth areas. I want to make sure our dividend is stable. And the third option there is the share repurchases."
  • Robert Andrew Terrell, Stephens: Questioned M&A considerations. Harrison stated, "We're not adverse to considering options, but we don't have anything we're looking at currently, but we're always out there talking to people as far as potentials for doing things with our capital."
  • Kelly Ann Motta, KBW: Asked about the impact of tax law changes. Moses answered, "The outlook for the rest of the year is 23.2% on that tax rate."
  • Timur Felixovich Braziler, Wells Fargo: Probed on criticized assets and asset growth. Nakamura replied, "For the most part, they will end up curing themselves...we are confident in our book. The book is strong."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on clarifications around loan growth, margin outlook, and credit trends, with no overtly skeptical or negative lines of questioning.
  • Management’s tone during prepared remarks was confident, emphasizing strong results and credit quality, with Harrison stating, "We are not observing any broad signs of weakness across either the consumer or commercial portfolios."
  • During Q&A, management remained consistent but occasionally acknowledged uncertainties, particularly regarding tariffs and loan growth, using phrases like "it's a bit of a challenge for balances" and "we don't know exactly what's going to happen."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analyst tone remained stable, with management showing slightly more caution in forward guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from low to mid-single digit loan growth to low single digits for the full year.
  • Loan balances moved from a decline of $115 million in Q1 to an increase of $59 million in Q2, with stabilization in floor plan balances and continued strength in C&I.
  • Management’s tone became marginally more cautious, particularly around loan growth and external economic variables like tariffs and tourism trends.
  • Analysts continued to probe around margin sustainability, capital deployment, and asset quality, reflecting ongoing interest in balance sheet optimization and risk.
  • Expense guidance improved, with Moses now expecting full-year expenses to be around $506 million, better than previously anticipated.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited uncertainty regarding tariffs, which impact dealer floor plan balances and potentially broader credit demand.
  • Harrison noted ongoing softness in Japanese and Canadian tourism, though U.S. arrivals remained strong.
  • Classified assets increased, but Nakamura described these as "well secured" and not indicative of broad credit deterioration.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of recent loan growth and the competitive environment for construction loan takeouts.
  • Management reiterated conservative reserve levels and close monitoring of criticized assets.

Final Takeaway

First Hawaiian delivered a quarter marked by broad-based earnings improvement, stable credit quality, and prudent cost management. Management signaled a slightly more cautious outlook on loan growth, now guiding to low single digits for the year, while highlighting resilience in core deposit franchises and ongoing share repurchases. With a stable margin outlook and ongoing vigilance on credit and external risks, the company remains focused on disciplined capital deployment and maintaining strong fundamentals as it navigates a changing operating environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. drilling rigs down for 12th time in 13 weeks, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of total oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. fell by 2 to 542, resuming their decline after last week's gain followed 11 consecutive weekly declines, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest survey.

Oil rigs dropped 7 to 415 this week, the lowest count since September 2021, while gas rigs added 5 to 122, their highest since August 2023, and the total rig count was down 47 rigs, or 8% below this time last year.

In Texas, the total rig count fell by 4 to 249, the lowest since October 2021; the total rig count in the Permian Basin - which also includes eastern New Mexico - fell by 3 to 260, the lowest since September 2021, and rigs in the Eagle Ford shale in south Texas shed 2 to 39, the lowest since October 2021.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Monster earnings week includes Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Mastercard
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - July 28

 

  • Volatility watch - Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) and Krispy Kreme (DNUT) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume and a surge in retail interest. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include NextSource (OTCQB:NSRCF), Aureus Greenway (AGH), and TE Connectivity (TEL). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Badger Meter (BMI), Molina (MOH), and Centene (CNC).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Welltower (WELL), Waste Management (WM), and Enterprise Products (EPD).
  • IPO watch - Ambiq Micro (AMBQ) is expected to price its IPO and begin to trade next week. The company develops ultra-low power semiconductor solutions for edge AI and general computing applications. Figma (FIG) is also expected to start trading. The design software company expects to sell about 37 million shares at $25 to $28 each, which would generate as much as $1 billion in proceeds. The IPO lockup period expires for certain blocks of shares of Smithfield Foods (SFD). The analyst quiet period expires on CoastalSouth Bancshares (COSO), CapsoVision (CV), Grande Group (GRAN), and Empro Group (EMPG) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Bank of Montreal (BMO), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Morgan Stanley (MS), and Costco (COST).
  • All day - Target (TGT) officially ends its price-matching program after a ten-year run of matching prices that customers found for the same item at Amazon (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT).

 

Tuesday - July 29

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Visa (V), Procter & Gamble (PG), Unitedhealth Group (UNH), Merck (MRK), Booking Holdings (BKNG), Boeing (BA), Spotify (SPOT), Starbucks (SBUX), and Paypal (PYPL). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Teladoc Health (TDOC) and Avis Budget (CAR) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is scheduled to meet Chinese counterparts in Stockholm for further trade talks.
  • All day - Veru (VERU) will be participating in one-on-one meetings and a fireside chat presentation with investors at the BTIG Virtual Biotech Conference.

 

Wednesday - July 30

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), Qualcomm (QCOM), Altria Group (MO), Ford Motor (F), and Arm Holdings (ARM). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Vita Coco (COCO), Carvana (CVNA), and Wingstop (WING) after their earnings are released.
  • All day- It is the five-year anniversary since XPeng (XPEV) went public. The EV stock traded about 30% above its IPO pricing level at the time of publication.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve's FOMC will issue its monetary policy statement.
  • 2:30 p.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will hold a press confernce.

 

Thursday - July 31

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), Mastercard (MA), Abbvie (ABBV), Comcast (CMCSA), Coinbase (COIN), Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), and Strategy (MSTR). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Reddit (RDDT), Roblox (RBLX), and Cloudflare (NET) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - The U.S. Federal Circuit Court of Appeals will hold hearings on the Court of International Trade's ruling on International Economic Emergency Powers Act tariffs. If the court upholds the lower court’s ruling that IEEPA does not grant the President the authority to use tariffs due to persistent trade deficits, it could introduce more uncertainty into trade negotiations.
  • All day - Shareholders with NV5 Global (NVEE) and Acuren (TIC) will vote on the planned $1.7B merger between the two companies.
  • 10:00 a.m. - The core PCE price index report for June will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.7% year-over-year in May.
  • 4:00 p.m. Amazon (AMZN) will hold its earnings call. Key themes for the earnings update and conference call with management include AI innovation with AWS’s AI-powered services under scrutiny and efficiency gains in logistics. Investors are also watching for margin trends in AWS, as increased capital spending could affect profitability. Major suppliers to the e-commerce giant with a strong post-earnings trading correlation to it include Aterian (ATER), NovaBay Pharma (NBY), and Air Transport (ATSG). Options trading implies a 5.5% swing for Amazon following the report. A wildcard would be an announcement of a hike in the price of Amazon Prime, with the company approaching four years since the last increase.
  • 4:00 p.m. - Apple (AAPL) will hold its earnings call. Major topics will be the update on the company's AI roadmap and how the company is managing tariffs and supply chain issues. Suppliers to Apple (AAPL) that historically move directionally with the stock on earnings day include Skyworks (SWKS), Take-Two Interactive (TTWO), and Qorvo (QRVO).

 

Friday - August 1

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Exxon Mobile (XOM), Chevron (CVX), Enbridge (ENB), and Colgate-Palmolive (CL).
  • All day - It is the tariff deadline date for various U.S. trading partners.
  • All day - Data reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports. Chinese electric vehicle makers NIO (NIO), XPeng (XPEV), Li Auto (LI), BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF), and ZEEKR (ZK) will also issue monthly deliveries reports.
  • 8:30 a.m. The July Jobs Report will be released. Economists expect 101K job additions, and the unemployment rate is forecast to tick higher to 4.2%.

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Joshua Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: LMVH in discussions to sell Marc Jacobs - WSJ
data.description:
LVMH Moët Hennessy (OTCPK:LVMHF) is in talks to sell its Marc Jacobs fashion brand in a deal valued at about $1 billion.

The luxury behemoth has been talking to several parties about a deal, including Reebok owner Authentic, according to a WSJ report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Brookstone owner Bluestar Alliance and Vera Wang parent WHP Global are also potential buyers.  

A deal may happen soon as long as talks don't fall apart, according to the report.

data.title: Wall Street pushes higher after S&P 500 and Nasdaq tagged fresh closing highs
data.description:
Wall Street moves higher on Friday as investors digest the previous session’s record-setting finishes for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) has advanced by 0.4% as it conitues to eye its own record high. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has moved up by 0.4% and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has gained 0.4% so far on the session. 

From a sector perspective, eight of the S&P 500’s 11 segments are in the green, led by strong performances in Materialsy and Industrials. On the flip side, Real Estate is lagging behind as the weakest performer of the day. 

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields are relatively muted with the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rising by 1 basis point to 4.41%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) sits near even at 3.91%. 

Looking out at the economic calendar, durable goods dropped less than feared in June, with new orders for manufactured durable goods: -9.3% M/M to $311.8B vs. -10.4% consensus and +16.5% prior (revised from +16.4%).

“As it stands, hard economic data in the US has shown signs of weakness over the past couple of quarters. But, contrary to the belief of many, the US economy remains robust and far from recessionary levels,” Seeking Alpha analyst Christopher Yates stated.

On the earnings end, Intel (INTC) shares are down 9.4% after the company's bottom line in the second quarter missed Wall Street estimates. The firm's Q3 outlook also failed to impress investors. The company also announced a 15% workforce reduction.

data.title: Bayer's Elinzanetant review period gets extended by FDA
data.description:
Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) (OTCPK:BAYRY) (OTCPK:BAMXF) said it has received notification from the FDA regarding an extension of the review period for the marketing application of its experimental drug, Elinzanetant, to treat menopausal hot flash therapy.

The FDA has indicated that more time is necessary to thoroughly review the NDA submission, which includes additional information supporting the application.

The review period extension could last up to 90 days. The FDA has not expressed any concerns about the overall approval of Elinzanetant in its communications.

data.title: Henry Schein downgraded at Stifel on market share concerns
data.description:
Henry Schein (NASDAQ:HSIC) shares traded lower on Friday after Stifel downgraded the dental product distributor to Hold from Buy, citing potential market share losses for the company’s consumables business.

Analyst Jonathan Block cited an ongoing shift in the purchasing patterns for dental consumables. He pointed out that a Stifel-commissioned survey among general dentists indicated a maximum share of 31% for online vendors in the market for consumable distributor purchases, twice the current level.

In terms of market share impact, the analyst noted that Henry Schein (NASDAQ:HSIC) was the biggest loser in its survey, with most losses attributed to online channels. He projects HSIC’s current market share to drop by 130 bps to 35.2% over the next one to two years.

“Our checks suggest the online trend may continue for many more years, weighing on Henry Schein’s results,” the analyst wrote, trimming the stock’s target price to $75 from $80 per share.

As for valuation, Block argued that Henry Schein’s (NASDAQ:HSIC) premium in terms of NTM earnings per share is a “concern,” as the multiple stands above the stock’s three-year average and compared to its peers. “In light of our share diligence, the current premium is a concern,” he wrote.
